abstract: Tells the delegate that the specified accessory was disconnected from the device.
overview:
## Discussion

The accessory manager calls this method as a convenience whenever it receives an [doc://com.apple.externalaccessory/documentation/ExternalAccessory/EAAccessoryDidDisconnectNotification] notification. You can use this method to remove any references to the specified accessory object and to stop any services currently using the accessory.

Because this is a convenience method, your delegate does not also need to register as an observer of the [doc://com.apple.externalaccessory/documentation/ExternalAccessory/EAAccessoryDidDisconnectNotification] notification. However, if you want your delegate to be notified of newly connected accessories, you should configure it as an observer of the [doc://com.apple.externalaccessory/documentation/ExternalAccessory/EAAccessoryDidConnectNotification] notification.

# accessoryDidDisconnect(_:)

**Instance Method**

Tells the delegate that the specified accessory was disconnected from the device.

## Declaration

```swift
optional func accessoryDidDisconnect(_ accessory: EAAccessory)
```

## Parameters

- **accessory**: The accessory that was disconnected.

## Discussion

The accessory manager calls this method as a convenience whenever it receives an [doc://com.apple.externalaccessory/documentation/ExternalAccessory/EAAccessoryDidDisconnectNotification] notification. You can use this method to remove any references to the specified accessory object and to stop any services currently using the accessory.

Because this is a convenience method, your delegate does not also need to register as an observer of the [doc://com.apple.externalaccessory/documentation/ExternalAccessory/EAAccessoryDidDisconnectNotification] notification. However, if you want your delegate to be notified of newly connected accessories, you should configure it as an observer of the [doc://com.apple.externalaccessory/documentation/ExternalAccessory/EAAccessoryDidConnectNotification] notification.
title: accessoryDidDisconnect(_:)
